stack: Java / Apache Tomcat servlet app (faithful stdlib-Python mock)

vulns.desc: Native Java deserialization -> RCE: the app round-trips a base64 Java serialized object (stream magic AC ED 00 05 = base64 'rO0AB') in the JSESSIONOBJ cookie and accepts one at POST /api/restore, calling the equivalent of ObjectInputStream.readObject() on attacker-controlled bytes. A ysoserial gadget chain (CommonsCollections / TemplatesImpl / etc.) achieves remote code execution.
vulns.exploit: POST /api/restore a base64 ysoserial CommonsCollections payload (rO0AB...) -> the gadget chain is recognised and would execute via readObject() -> RCE. Also reachable by tampering the JSESSIONOBJ cookie.

vulns.desc: Default Tomcat Manager credentials: /manager/html accepts default credentials (tomcat/tomcat, admin/admin, manager/manager). Authenticated manager access allows deploying a malicious WAR -> RCE.
vulns.exploit: GET /manager/html with Authorization: Basic dG9tY2F0OnRvbWNhdA== (tomcat:tomcat) -> Tomcat Web Application Manager.

vulns.desc: Verbose Java stack traces: malformed input to the deserialization endpoint returns a full Java exception (java.io.InvalidClassException / ObjectInputStream.readObject with line numbers and internal class/package names), disclosing the stack and internals.
vulns.exploit: POST /api/restore with non-serialized bytes -> java.io.InvalidClassException stack trace in the response.

vulns.desc: End-of-life / outdated stack disclosed: responses send Server: Apache-Coyote/1.1 and X-Powered-By: ... (Apache Tomcat/7.0.42, Java/1.8.0_71) — both end-of-life and unpatched, with public deserialization-related CVEs (CVE-2016-8735, etc.). Version disclosure via banner reveals the EOL software.
vulns.exploit: curl -I / -> Server + X-Powered-By banners disclosing Tomcat 7.0.42 / Java 1.8.0_71.

vulns.desc: Missing security headers on all responses: no Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors, X-Content-Type-Options, or HSTS.
vulns.exploit: curl -I any endpoint -> none of CSP/XFO/XCTO/HSTS present.
